mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital for businesses and encourages investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS has been declining since late 2007, currently at 2.01%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 2.5% and declining over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, which reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE has been rising steadily in 2008, currently at 5.7%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad-based weakening of consumer demand and investment, signaling a severe economic downturn that will negatively impact corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% MoM AND HOUST declines > 5% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:Both RSAFS and HOUST show recent weakness as of 2008-08-15
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST meet their respective decline thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic expansion, robust industrial production growth indicates strong corporate activity and demand for goods, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. This effect reverses during contractions.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO growth and S&P 500 5-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) during periods identified as economic expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration or contraction in real GDP growth indicates a weakening overall economy, which directly translates to lower corporate revenues and profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 growth has slowed considerably in recent quarters, last reported at 11700.6
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where annualized GDPC1 growth falls below 1% QoQ.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in non-farm payrolls signals a contraction in employment, reducing aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown recent declines, last reported at 137615.0
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods following two consecutive months of PAYEMS decline.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high and rising inflation erodes purchasing power and increases input costs for businesses, leading to lower profit margins and a negative outlook for equities, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL is currently high at 219.181, indicating significant inflation
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and sustained decline in housing starts reflects a significant downturn in the housing market and broader economic confidence, which negatively impacts related industries and consumer wealth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST has been declining significantly in 2008, last reported at 1066.0
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 45% of periods where HOUST declines by more than 10% over three months.
